sections.paragraph:
f. plur. (from the root יָצָא to go out)-

(1) a going out, metaph. a going forth from danger, deliverance, Psa 68:21 compare the root, Ecc 7:18.

(2) the place from which (any person or thing) goes forth, hence a gate, Eze 48:30 a fountain, Pro 4:23, תֹּוצְאֹות חַיִּים “the fountain of life,” of happiness; also the place of the exit or termination of anything, Num 34:4, Num 34:5, Num 34:8, Num 34:9 Jos 15:4.
